_meta.format: plain-text. Bullet formula: 'Built A doing B using C, which produced D — quantify D'. Buzzwords front-loaded. No professional summary. No new-grad language.
_meta.positioning: Software engineer with production deployments, open-source contributions, and research publications. Builds API platforms, AI workflows, and cloud infrastructure.
_meta.usage: v2.3: use data.work=[{id,bullets[]}] in build scripts. Work IDs: job1|job2|job3|job4. All bullets pre-validated at 116 chars max. Do NOT add bullets outside this file without running validate.py.
bullet_rules: Max 116 chars WITH spaces — hard limit, guaranteed 1-line at 11pt Calibri, Letter, 0.5in margins
Tagline max 76 chars WITH spaces — hard limit, 1-line at 12pt Calibri
Project header (name + ' | ' + stack + GitHub + date) max 116 chars — hard limit
Lead with varied non-repeated action verbs — no two bullets in one resume should start with the same verb
Lead with the most ATS-important keyword or tool
IMPACT IS MANDATORY: every bullet must answer 'so what?' — add before/after, time saved, % improvement, or business outcome. Activity-only bullets (no outcome) are disallowed.
BUSINESS CONTEXT: at least 1 bullet per work experience should explain WHY the work mattered — what did the team/user/business gain? Not just what was built, but the consequence.
ACTIVITY-ONLY RED FLAGS — always rewrite: 'Collaborated with...', 'Participated in...', 'Assisted with...', 'Worked on...' unless followed by a concrete quantified outcome.
Research bullets: state PURPOSE + ACHIEVEMENT + IMPACT — no fabrication
Do not fabricate stack items, metrics, or features not actually built
Bullets should tell a story a non-technical HM can understand
Use short_stack field for project header (3-4 most ATS-important techs, ≤40 chars)
tagline_rules: AVOID generic 'experienced in Tech1, Tech2, Tech3, Tech4' — it differentiates nothing
USE value-oriented format: '{Job Title} building {what} with {tech}' or '{Job Title} — {achievement/differentiator}'
GOOD: 'Software Engineer building REST APIs in Python and Go with cloud automation' (73c)
GOOD: 'Backend Engineer — 4+ prod features/sprint; FastAPI, distributed systems' (71c)
BAD: 'Software Engineer experienced in Python, REST APIs, Go, and Cloud Services' (generic, no proof)

contact.name: Alex Chen
contact.work_auth: Authorized to work in the US.

tagline_note: DO NOT use 'experienced in Tech1, Tech2, Tech3, Tech4' — that is generic and differentiates nothing. Use a VALUE-ORIENTED format: '{Job Title} building {what you build} with {tech1} and {tech2}' OR '{Job Title} — {brief proof-point or differentiator}'. Keep exact job title from JD. ≤76 chars. Never use 'graduate' or 'new grad' language.

resume_rules.no_fabrication: NEVER add tools, metrics, features, or claims not in this file. Cross-check project stack ground truth before writing bullets.
resume_rules.action_verb_rule: No two bullets in a single resume should start with the same action verb. Use varied verbs: Built, Designed, Engineered, Developed, Automated, Configured, Implemented, Deployed, Wrote, Contributed, Shipped, Optimized, Integrated, Orchestrated, Scaffolded, Diagnosed, Collaborated, Analyzed.
resume_rules.para_count: Dynamic (v2.3+). Typical: 3-job x 5b + 3-proj x 3b = 44. Check actual output in Word.
resume_rules.format: DOCX via buildv2.js (v2.3+). data.work=[{id,bullets[]}]. Work IDs: job1|job2|job3|job4.
resume_rules.buzzwords_front: Lead each bullet with the most important/searchable keyword or technology
resume_rules.tools_in_bullets: Every bullet should mention at least one specific tool/tech used
resume_rules.3_job_layout: Use job1 + job2 + job3 for most roles. Add job4 for academic/research roles. 3-job + 3-proj x 3b = 44 paras (likely 1-page; verify in Word).

fabrication_warnings.paper1_is_rl_NOT_coq: IEEE 2024 paper = Adversarial RL robustness + entropy-based detection. 2nd author. NSF-funded. NOT about Coq or formal verification.
fabrication_warnings.coq_paper_is_NOT_published: Coq paper = Program Graphs to Proofs. 1st author. DRAFT — not yet published. NOT published at the IEEE conference.
fabrication_warnings.mrr_is_python_NOT_csharp: Revenue Analytics Dashboard = Python/FastAPI/BigQuery. HomeBoard = C#/ASP.NET Core 8.
fabrication_warnings.no_fabricated_metrics: Never add metrics not in this file — ask the candidate for actual before/after numbers.

quality_review._note: Bullets flagged as activity-only or missing business context. Improve when real numbers are available — DO NOT fabricate.

job1_collaborated.current: Collaborated with senior engineers on code review, iterating Ruby implementations to meet production standards
job1_collaborated.problem: Activity-only — 'to meet production standards' is vague. No outcome.
job1_collaborated.improve_to: Collaborated with senior engineers on code review, refactoring Ruby pipeline logic to cut [X] edge-case failures per week
job1_collaborated.needs_from_candidate: What specific improvement came out of the code review cycles? Bug rate? Performance? Latency?

job2_runbooks.current: Wrote runbooks for distributed simulation systems; reduced onboarding time and improved cross-team handoffs
job2_runbooks.problem: Vague — 'reduced onboarding time' by how much? From what to what?
job2_runbooks.improve_to: Wrote runbooks for 3 distributed simulation systems, cutting new-engineer onboarding from [X days] to [Y hours]
job2_runbooks.needs_from_candidate: Actual before/after onboarding time. Even approximate is fine.
